seoTitle: Record an Owner's Draw in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to record an owner's draw in QuickBooks Online so personal withdrawals stay separate from business capital and your records stay clean.
intro: Withdrawing money from your business for personal use is common, but recording it incorrectly can distort your company's financial picture. This guide covers how to record an owner's draw in QuickBooks Online, keeping personal withdrawals clearly separated from business expenses. It's meant for small business owners and freelancers who want accurate books without messy categorization.

steps.heading: Set up an owner's draw account
steps.body: Create a dedicated equity account for owner's draws if one doesn't already exist in your chart of accounts.

steps.heading: Record the withdrawal
steps.body: Enter the transaction as a transfer or expense tied to the owner's draw account rather than a regular business expense.

steps.heading: Keep personal and business finances separate
steps.body: Make sure the draw doesn't get miscategorized as a business cost, which would distort your profit and loss statement.

steps.heading: Review equity impact
steps.body: Check your balance sheet afterward to see how the draw affects the owner's equity in the business.

steps.heading: Repeat consistently for future draws
steps.body: Use the same process each time you withdraw funds so your records stay consistent and easy to review.

faq.question: Is an owner's draw considered a business expense?
faq.answer: No, an owner's draw is a withdrawal of equity, not a deductible business expense, so it should be recorded separately.

faq.question: Does recording an owner's draw affect my taxes?
faq.answer: Owner's draws are generally not taxed as business expenses, but tax treatment can vary — it's best to confirm with an accountant.

faq.question: Can I automate recurring owner's draws?
faq.answer: Yes, if you take regular draws, you can set up a recurring transaction tied to your owner's draw account.

faq.question: What's the difference between an owner's draw and a salary?
faq.answer: A draw is a withdrawal of equity typically used by sole proprietors, while a salary is a set wage often used in corporations.
